There is nothing wrong with a person being praised and rewarded."' He said: I saw Abū al-Dardāʾ delighted by this, raising his head toward him and saying, 'Did you hear that from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?' He would say, 'Yes.' He kept repeating this until I thought he would kneel before him.
iwh_id: ʿAbd al-Malik ibn ʿAmr Abū ʿĀmir menceritakan kepada kami seraya berkata: Hishām ibn Saʿd menceritakan kepada kami seraya berkata: Qays ibn Bishr al-Taghlibī menceritakan kepada kami seraya berkata: My father memberitahukan kepadaku — and he biasa sit with Abū al-Dardāʾ — dia berkata: There was in Damascus a man from para sahabat of Nabi ﷺ called Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah, and he was a solitary man who rarely sat with people. He was always in prayer, and when he finished, he would glorify and magnify Allah until he went to his family. One day he passed by us while we were with Abū al-Dardāʾ. Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and does not harm you.' Dia berkata, 'Rasulullah ﷺ sent out a military detachment, and they returned. A man from among them came and sat in the gathering where Rasulullah ﷺ was, and said to a man next to him, "If you had seen us when we met the enemy! So-and-so charged and thrust his spear and berkata, 'Take that, and I am the Ghifārī lad!' What do you think of his statement?" Dia berkata, "I think he has nullified his reward." Another man heard this and berkata, "I see nothing wrong with that." They debated until Nabi ﷺ heard them and berkata, "Subhanallah! There is nothing wrong with a person being praised and rewarded."' Dia berkata: I saw Abū al-Dardāʾ delighted by this, raising his head toward him and seraya berkata, 'Did you hear that dari Rasulullah ﷺ?' He would say, 'Yes.' He kept repeating this until I thought he would kneel before him.
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْمَلِكِ بْنُ عَمْرٍو أَبُو عَامِرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا قَيْسُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ التَّغْلِبِيُّ قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي أَبِي وَكَانَ جَلِيسًا لِأَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ قَالَ كَانَ بِدِمَشْقَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُقَالُ لَهُ ابْنُ الْحَنْظَلِيَّةِ وَكَانَ رَجُلًا مُتَوَحِّدًا قَلَّمَا يُجَالِسُ النَّاسَ إِنَّمَا هُوَ فِي صَلَاةٍ فَإِذَا فَرَغَ فَإِنَّمَا يُسَبِّحُ وَيُكَبِّرُ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ أَهْلَهُ فَمَرَّ بِنَا يَوْمًا وَنَحْنُ عِنْدَ أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ بَعَثَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ سَرِيَّةً فَقَدِمْتُ فَجَاءَ رَجُلٌ مِنْهُمْ فَجَلَسَ ف
matn_ar: ِي الْمَجْلِسِ الَّذِي فِيهِ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ لِرَجُلٍ إِلَى جَنْبِهِ لَوْ رَأَيْتَنَا حِينَ الْتَقَيْنَا نَحْنُ وَالْعَدُوَّ فَحَمَلَ فُلَانٌ فَطَعَنَ فَقَالَ خُذْهَا وَأَنَا الْغُلَامُ الْغِفَارِيُّ كَيْفَ تَرَى فِي قَوْلِهِ قَالَ مَا أُرَاهُ إِلَّا قَدْ أَبْطَلَ أَجْرَهُ فَسَمِعَ ذَلِكَ آخَرُ فَقَالَ مَا أَرَى بِذَلِكَ بَأْسًا فَتَنَازَعَا حَتَّى سَمِعَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يُحْمَدَ وَيُؤْجَرَ قَالَ فَرَأَيْتُ أَبَا الدَّرْدَاءِ سُرَّ بِذَلِكَ وَجَعَلَ يَرْفَعُ رَأْسَهُ إِلَيْهِ وَيَقُولُ آنْتَ سَمِعْتَ ذَلِكَ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَيَقُولُ نَعَمْ فَمَا زَالَ يُعِيدُ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى إِنِّي لَأَقُولُ لَيَبْرُكَنَّ عَلَى رُكْبَتَيْهِ
matn_ar_clean: ي المجلس الذي فيه رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فقال لرجل إلى جنبه لو رأيتنا حين التقينا نحن والعدو فحمل فلان فطعن فقال خذها وأنا الغلام الغفاري كيف ترى في قوله قال ما أراه إلا قد أبطل أجره فسمع ذلك آخر فقال ما أرى بذلك بأسا فتنازعا حتى سمع النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم فقال سبحان الله لا بأس أن يحمد ويؤجر قال فرأيت أبا الدرداء سر بذلك وجعل يرفع رأسه إليه ويقول آنت سمعت ذلك من رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فيقول نعم فما زال يعيد عليه حتى إني لأقول ليبركن على ركبتيه

isnad_chain.name_ar: عبد الملك بن عمرو أبو عامر

iwh_en: ʿAbd al-Malik ibn ʿAmr Abū ʿĀmir narrated to us saying: Hishām ibn Saʿd narrated to us saying: Qays ibn Bishr al-Taghlibī narrated to us saying: My father told me — and he used to sit with Abū al-Dardāʾ — he said: There was in Damascus a man from the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ called Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah, and he was a solitary man who rarely sat with people. He was always in prayer, and when he finished, he would glorify and magnify Allah until he went to his family. One day he passed by us while we were with Abū al-Dardāʾ. Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said, 'the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent out a military detachment, and they returned. A man from among them came and sat in the gathering where the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was, and said to a man next to him, "If you had seen us when we met the enemy! So-and-so charged and thrust his spear and said, 'Take that, and I am the Ghifārī lad!' What do you think of his statement?" He said, "I think he has nullified his reward." Another man heard this and said, "I see nothing wrong with that." They debated until the Prophet ﷺ heard them and said, "Glory be to Allah! There is nothing wrong with a person being praised and rewarded."' He said: I saw Abū al-Dardāʾ delighted by this, raising his head toward him and saying, 'Did you hear that from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?' He would say, 'Yes.' He kept repeating this until I thought he would kneel before him.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ قَالَ لَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ النَّفَقَةَ عَلَى الْخَيْلِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ كَبَاسِطِ يَدِهِ ‏ ‏بِالصَّدَقَةِ ‏ ‏لَا يَقْبِضُهَا ‏
ar_clean: قَالَ ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ قَالَ لَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ إِنَّ النَّفَقَةَ عَلَى الْخَيْلِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ كَبَاسِطِ يَدِهِ بِالصَّدَقَةِ لَا يَقْبِضُهَا
sunnah_ref_en: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'Spending on horses in the cause of Allah is like one who extends his hand in charity and does not withhold it.'
iwh_id: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and tidaklah harm you.' He berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, 'Spending on horses in the cause of Allah is like one who extends his hand in charity and tidaklah withhold it.'
matn_ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ قَالَ لَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ النَّفَقَةَ عَلَى الْخَيْلِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ كَبَاسِطِ يَدِهِ ‏ ‏بِالصَّدَقَةِ ‏ ‏لَا يَقْبِضُهَا ‏
matn_ar_clean: ‏ ‏قال ‏ ‏ثم مر بنا يوما آخر فقال له ‏ ‏أبو الدرداء ‏ ‏كلمة تنفعنا ولا تضرك قال قال لنا رسول الله ‏ ‏صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏ ‏إن النفقة على الخيل في سبيل الله كباسط يده ‏ ‏بالصدقة ‏ ‏لا يقبضها ‏
iwh_en: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'Spending on horses in the cause of Allah is like one who extends his hand in charity and does not withhold it.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ فَقَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نِعْمَ الرَّجُلُ ‏ ‏خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ ‏ ‏لَوْلَا طُولُ ‏ ‏جُمَّتِهِ ‏ ‏وَإِسْبَالُ ‏ ‏إِزَارِهِ ‏ ‏فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ ‏ ‏خُرَيْمًا ‏ ‏فَجَعَلَ يَأْخُذُ ‏ ‏شَفْرَةً ‏ ‏يَقْطَعُ بِهَا شَعَرَهُ إِلَى أَنْصَافِ أُذُنَيْهِ وَرَفَعَ ‏ ‏إِزَارَهُ ‏ ‏إِلَى أَنْصَافِ سَاقَيْهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فَأَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏دَخَلْتُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ عَلَى ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏فَإِذَا عِنْدَهُ شَيْخٌ ‏ ‏جُمَّتُهُ ‏ ‏فَوْقَ أُذُنَيْهِ ‏ ‏وَرِدَاؤُهُ ‏ ‏إِلَى سَاقَيْهِ فَسَأَلْتُ عَنْهُ فَقَالُوا هَذَا ‏ ‏خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ ‏
ar_clean: قَالَ ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ فَقَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نِعْمَ الرَّجُلُ خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ لَوْلَا طُولُ جُمَّتِهِ وَإِسْبَالُ إِزَارِهِ فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ خُرَيْمًا فَجَعَلَ يَأْخُذُ شَفْرَةً يَقْطَعُ بِهَا شَعَرَهُ إِلَى أَنْصَافِ أُذُنَيْهِ وَرَفَعَ إِزَارَهُ إِلَى أَنْصَافِ سَاقَيْهِ قَالَ فَأَخْبَرَنِي أَبِي قَالَ دَخَلْتُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ عَلَى مُعَاوِيَةَ فَإِذَا عِنْدَهُ شَيْخٌ جُمَّتُهُ فَوْقَ أُذُنَيْهِ وَرِدَاؤُهُ إِلَى سَاقَيْهِ فَسَأَلْتُ عَنْهُ فَقَالُوا هَذَا خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ
sunnah_ref_en: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'What an excellent man Khuraym al-Asadī is, were it not for the length of his hair and the trailing of his lower garment.' When this reached Khuraym, he hastily took a blade and cut his hair to the level of his ears and raised his lower garment to mid-shin. My father told me: I later visited Muʿāwiyah, and there was an elderly man with him whose hair was above his ears and his garment reached his shins. I asked about him, and they said, 'This is Khuraym al-Asadī.'
iwh_id: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and tidaklah harm you.' He berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, 'What an excellent man Khuraym al-Asadī is, were it not for the length of his hair and the trailing of his lower garment.' When this reached Khuraym, he hastily took a blade and cut his hair to the level of his ears and raised his lower garment to mid-shin. My father memberitahukan kepadaku: I later visited Muʿāwiyah, and there was an elderly man with him whose hair was above his ears and his garment reached his shins. aku bertanya about him, and mereka berkata, 'This is Khuraym al-Asadī.'
isnad_ar: قَالَ ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ فَقَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نِعْمَ الرَّجُلُ خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ لَوْلَا طُولُ جُمَّتِهِ وَإِسْبَالُ إِزَارِه
matn_ar: ِ فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ خُرَيْمًا فَجَعَلَ يَأْخُذُ شَفْرَةً يَقْطَعُ بِهَا شَعَرَهُ إِلَى أَنْصَافِ أُذُنَيْهِ وَرَفَعَ إِزَارَهُ إِلَى أَنْصَافِ سَاقَيْهِ قَالَ فَأَخْبَرَنِي أَبِي قَالَ دَخَلْتُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ عَلَى مُعَاوِيَةَ فَإِذَا عِنْدَهُ شَيْخٌ جُمَّتُهُ فَوْقَ أُذُنَيْهِ وَرِدَاؤُهُ إِلَى سَاقَيْهِ فَسَأَلْتُ عَنْهُ فَقَالُوا هَذَا خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ
matn_ar_clean: فبلغ ذلك خريما فجعل يأخذ شفرة يقطع بها شعره إلى أنصاف أذنيه ورفع إزاره إلى أنصاف ساقيه قال فأخبرني أبي قال دخلت بعد ذلك على معاوية فإذا عنده شيخ جمته فوق أذنيه ورداؤه إلى ساقيه فسألت عنه فقالوا هذا خريم الأسدي
iwh_en: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'What an excellent man Khuraym al-Asadī is, were it not for the length of his hair and the trailing of his lower garment.' When this reached Khuraym, he hastily took a blade and cut his hair to the level of his ears and raised his lower garment to mid-shin. My father told me: I later visited Muʿāwiyah, and there was an elderly man with him whose hair was above his ears and his garment reached his shins. I asked about him, and they said, 'This is Khuraym al-Asadī.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ وَنَحْنُ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ فَقَالَ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏إِنَّكُمْ قَادِمُونَ عَلَى إِخْوَانِكُمْ فَأَصْلِحُوا ‏ ‏رِحَالَكُمْ ‏ ‏وَأَصْلِحُوا لِبَاسَكُمْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لَا يُحِبُّ ‏ ‏الْفُحْشَ ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏التَّفَحُّشَ ‏
ar_clean: قَالَ ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ وَنَحْنُ عِنْدَ أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ فَقَالَ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقُولُ إِنَّكُمْ قَادِمُونَ عَلَى إِخْوَانِكُمْ فَأَصْلِحُوا رِحَالَكُمْ وَأَصْلِحُوا لِبَاسَكُمْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لَا يُحِبُّ الْفُحْشَ وَلَا التَّفَحُّشَ
sunnah_ref_en: Then he passed by us another day while we were with Abū al-Dardāʾ, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, 'You are coming to your brethren, so fix your mounts and fix your clothing, so that you appear as a beauty mark among the people. Allah عز وجل does not love obscenity or deliberate obscenity.'
iwh_id: Then he passed by us another day while we were with Abū al-Dardāʾ, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and tidaklah harm you.' He berkata: aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ seraya berkata, 'You are coming to your brethren, so fix your mounts and fix your clothing, so that you appear as a beauty mark among the people. Allah عز وجل tidaklah love obscenity or deliberate obscenity.'
matn_ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ وَنَحْنُ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ فَقَالَ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏إِنَّكُمْ قَادِمُونَ عَلَى إِخْوَانِكُمْ فَأَصْلِحُوا ‏ ‏رِحَالَكُمْ ‏ ‏وَأَصْلِحُوا لِبَاسَكُمْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لَا يُحِبُّ ‏ ‏الْفُحْشَ ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏التَّفَحُّشَ ‏
matn_ar_clean: ‏ ‏قال ‏ ‏ثم مر بنا يوما آخر ونحن عند ‏ ‏أبي الدرداء ‏ ‏فقال له ‏ ‏أبو الدرداء ‏ ‏كلمة تنفعنا ولا تضرك فقال سمعت رسول الله ‏ ‏صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏ ‏يقول ‏ ‏إنكم قادمون على إخوانكم فأصلحوا ‏ ‏رحالكم ‏ ‏وأصلحوا لباسكم فإن الله عز وجل لا يحب ‏ ‏الفحش ‏ ‏ولا ‏ ‏التفحش ‏
iwh_en: Then he passed by us another day while we were with Abū al-Dardāʾ, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, 'You are coming to your brethren, so fix your mounts and fix your clothing, so that you appear as a beauty mark among the people. Allah عز وجل does not love obscenity or deliberate obscenity.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ أَبِي الرَّبِيعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏هُوَ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏الَّذِي رَوَى عَنْهُ ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏وَلَيْثُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏دَخَلْتُ مَسْجِدَ ‏ ‏دِمَشْقَ ‏ ‏فَرَأَيْتُ أُنَاسًا مُجْتَمِعِينَ وَشَيْخًا يُحَدِّثُهُمْ قُلْتُ مَنْ هَذَا قَالُوا ‏ ‏سَهْلُ ابْنُ الْحَنْظَلِيَّةِ ‏ ‏فَسَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَنْ أَكَلَ لَحْمًا فَلْيَتَوَضَّأْ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ صَالِحٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ أَبِي الرَّبِيعِ قَالَ أَبِي هُوَ سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الَّذِي رَوَى عَنْهُ شُعْبَةُ وَلَيْثُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ عَنْ الْقَاسِمِ مَوْلَى مُعَاوِيَةَ قَالَ دَخَلْتُ مَسْجِدَ دِمَشْقَ فَرَأَيْتُ أُنَاسًا مُجْتَمِعِينَ وَشَيْخًا يُحَدِّثُهُمْ قُلْتُ مَنْ هَذَا قَالُوا سَهْلُ ابْنُ الْحَنْظَلِيَّةِ فَسَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقُولُ مَنْ أَكَلَ لَحْمًا فَلْيَتَوَضَّأْ
sunnah_ref_en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us saying: Muʿāwiyah — meaning Ibn Ṣāliḥ — narrated to us, from Sulaymān Abū al-Rabīʿ. My father [Aḥmad] said: He is Sulaymān ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān, from whom Shuʿbah and Layth ibn Saʿd narrated. From al-Qāsim, the freed slave of Muʿāwiyah, who said: I entered the mosque of Damascus and saw people gathered with an elderly man narrating to them. I asked, 'Who is this?' They said, 'Sahl Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah.' I heard him saying: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, 'Whoever eats meat, let him perform wuḍūʾ.'
iwh_id: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī menceritakan kepada kami seraya berkata: Muʿāwiyah — yakni Ibn Ṣāliḥ — menceritakan kepada kami, dari Sulaymān Abū al-Rabīʿ. My father [Aḥmad] berkata: He is Sulaymān ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān, dari whom Shuʿbah and Layth ibn Saʿd meriwayatkan. From al-Qāsim, the freed slave of Muʿāwiyah, yang berkata: aku masuk the mosque of Damascus and saw people gathered with an elderly man narrating to them. aku bertanya, 'Who is this?' They berkata, 'Sahl Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah.' aku mendengar him seraya berkata: aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ seraya berkata, 'Whoever eats meat, let him perform wuḍūʾ.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ صَالِحٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ أَبِي الرَّبِيعِ قَالَ أَبِي هُوَ سُلَيْمَانُ بْ
matn_ar: نُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الَّذِي رَوَى عَنْهُ شُعْبَةُ وَلَيْثُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ عَنْ الْقَاسِمِ مَوْلَى مُعَاوِيَةَ قَالَ دَخَلْتُ مَسْجِدَ دِمَشْقَ فَرَأَيْتُ أُنَاسًا مُجْتَمِعِينَ وَشَيْخًا يُحَدِّثُهُمْ قُلْتُ مَنْ هَذَا قَالُوا سَهْلُ ابْنُ الْحَنْظَلِيَّةِ فَسَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقُولُ مَنْ أَكَلَ لَحْمًا فَلْيَتَوَضَّأْ
matn_ar_clean: ن عبد الرحمن الذي روى عنه شعبة وليث بن سعد عن القاسم مولى معاوية قال دخلت مسجد دمشق فرأيت أناسا مجتمعين وشيخا يحدثهم قلت من هذا قالوا سهل ابن الحنظلية فسمعته يقول سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول من أكل لحما فليتوضأ
iwh_en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us saying: Muʿāwiyah — meaning Ibn Ṣāliḥ — narrated to us, from Sulaymān Abū al-Rabīʿ. My father [Aḥmad] said: He is Sulaymān ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān, from whom Shuʿbah and Layth ibn Saʿd narrated. From al-Qāsim, the freed slave of Muʿāwiyah, who said: I entered the mosque of Damascus and saw people gathered with an elderly man narrating to them. I asked, 'Who is this?' They said, 'Sahl Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah.' I heard him saying: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, 'Whoever eats meat, let him perform wuḍūʾ.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏قَيْسُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ التَّغْلِبِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ جَلِيسًا ‏ ‏لِأَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏بِدِمَشْقَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ ‏ ‏بِدِمَشْقَ ‏ ‏رَجُلٌ يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْحَنْظَلِيَّةِ ‏ ‏مُتَوَحِّدًا لَا يَكَادُ يُكَلِّمُ أَحَدًا إِنَّمَا هُوَ فِي صَلَاةٍ فَإِذَا فَرَغَ يُسَبِّحُ وَيُكَبِّرُ وَيُهَلِّلُ حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ قَالَ فَمَرَّ عَلَيْنَا ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ وَنَحْنُ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏كَلِمَةً مِنْكَ تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ بَعَثَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏سَرِيَّةٍ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا أَنْ قَدِمْنَا جَلَسَ رَجُلٌ مِنْهُمْ فِي مَجْلِسٍ فِيهِ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ يَا فُلَانُ لَوْ رَأَيْتَ فُلَانًا طَعَنَ ثُمَّ قَالَ خُذْهَا وَأَنَا الْغُلَامُ ‏ ‏الْغِفَارِيُّ فَمَا ‏ ‏تَرَى قَالَ مَا أُرَاهُ إِلَّا قَدْ حَبِطَ أَجْرُهُ قَالَ فَتَكَلَّمُوا فِي ذَلِكَ حَتَّى سَمِعَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَصْوَاتَهُمْ فَقَالَ بَلْ ‏ ‏يُحْمَدُ وَيُؤْجَرُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فَسُرَّ بِذَلِكَ ‏ ‏أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏حَتَّى هَمَّ أَنْ ‏ ‏يَجْثُوَ ‏ ‏عَلَى رُكْبَتَيْهِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏آنْتَ سَمِعْتَهُ مِرَارًا قَالَ نَعَمْ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ حَدَّثَنِي قَيْسُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ التَّغْلِبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ وَكَانَ جَلِيسًا لِأَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ بِدِمَشْقَ قَالَ كَانَ بِدِمَشْقَ رَجُلٌ يُقَالُ لَهُ ابْنُ الْحَنْظَلِيَّةِ مُتَوَحِّدًا لَا يَكَادُ يُكَلِّمُ أَحَدًا إِنَّمَا هُوَ فِي صَلَاةٍ فَإِذَا فَرَغَ يُسَبِّحُ وَيُكَبِّرُ وَيُهَلِّلُ حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ قَالَ فَمَرَّ عَلَيْنَا ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ وَنَحْنُ عِنْدَ أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ كَلِمَةً مِنْكَ تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ بَعَثَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي سَرِيَّةٍ فَلَمَّا أَنْ قَدِمْنَا جَلَسَ رَجُلٌ مِنْهُمْ فِي مَجْلِسٍ فِيهِ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَقَالَ يَا فُلَانُ لَوْ رَأَيْتَ فُلَانًا طَعَنَ ثُمَّ قَالَ خُذْهَا وَأَنَا الْغُلَامُ الْغِفَارِيُّ فَمَا تَرَى قَالَ مَا أُرَاهُ إِلَّا قَدْ حَبِطَ أَجْرُهُ قَالَ فَتَكَلَّمُوا فِي ذَلِكَ حَتَّى سَمِعَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَصْوَاتَهُمْ فَقَالَ بَلْ يُحْمَدُ وَيُؤْجَرُ قَالَ فَسُرَّ بِذَلِكَ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ حَتَّى هَمَّ أَنْ يَجْثُوَ عَلَى رُكْبَتَيْهِ فَقَالَ آنْتَ سَمِعْتَهُ مِرَارًا قَالَ نَعَمْ
sunnah_ref_en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Hishām ibn Saʿd narrated to us, Qays ibn Bishr al-Taghlibī narrated to me, from his father — who used to sit with Abū al-Dardāʾ in Damascus — who said: There was in Damascus a man called Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah, a solitary man who hardly spoke to anyone. He was always in prayer, and when he finished, he would glorify, magnify, and declare the oneness of Allah until he returned to his family. One day he passed by us while we were with Abū al-Dardāʾ. Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word from you that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said, 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent us on a military detachment. When we returned, a man from among them sat in a gathering where the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was and said, "O so-and-so, if you had seen so-and-so thrust his spear and then say, 'Take that, and I am the Ghifārī lad!' — what do you think?" He said, "I think his reward has been nullified." They discussed this until the Prophet ﷺ heard their voices and said, "Rather, he is praised and rewarded."' Abū al-Dardāʾ was so delighted by this that he nearly knelt before him, saying, 'Did you hear that?' — repeating it several times, and he said, 'Yes.'
iwh_id: Wakīʿ menceritakan kepada kami, Hishām ibn Saʿd menceritakan kepada kami, Qays ibn Bishr al-Taghlibī menceritakan kepadaku, dari his father — who biasa sit with Abū al-Dardāʾ in Damascus — yang berkata: There was in Damascus a man called Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah, a solitary man who hardly spoke to anyone. He was always in prayer, and when he finished, he would glorify, magnify, and declare the oneness of Allah until he returned to his family. One day he passed by us while we were with Abū al-Dardāʾ. Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word from you that benefits us and does not harm you.' Dia berkata, 'Rasulullah ﷺ sent us on a military detachment. When we returned, a man from among them sat in a gathering where Rasulullah ﷺ was and berkata, "O so-and-so, if you had seen so-and-so thrust his spear and then say, 'Take that, and I am the Ghifārī lad!' — what do you think?" Dia berkata, "I think his reward has been nullified." They discussed this until Nabi ﷺ heard their voices and berkata, "Rather, he is praised and rewarded."' Abū al-Dardāʾ was so delighted by this that he nearly knelt before him, seraya berkata, 'Did you hear that?' — repeating it several times, and dia berkata, 'Yes.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ حَدَّثَنِي قَيْسُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ التَّغْلِبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ وَكَانَ جَلِيسًا لِأَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ بِدِمَشْقَ قَالَ كَانَ بِدِمَشْقَ رَجُلٌ يُقَالُ لَهُ ابْنُ الْحَنْظَلِيَّةِ مُتَوَحِّدًا لَا يَكَادُ يُكَلِّمُ أَحَدًا إِنَّمَا هُوَ فِي صَلَاةٍ فَإِذَا فَرَغَ يُسَبِّحُ وَيُكَبِّرُ وَيُهَلِّلُ حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ قَالَ فَمَرَّ عَلَيْنَا ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ وَنَحْنُ عِنْدَ أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ كَلِمَةً مِنْكَ تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ بَعَثَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي سَرِيَّةٍ فَلَمَّا أَنْ قَدِمْنَا جَلَسَ رَجُلٌ مِنْهُمْ فِي مَجْلِسٍ فِيهِ رَس
matn_ar: ُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَقَالَ يَا فُلَانُ لَوْ رَأَيْتَ فُلَانًا طَعَنَ ثُمَّ قَالَ خُذْهَا وَأَنَا الْغُلَامُ الْغِفَارِيُّ فَمَا تَرَى قَالَ مَا أُرَاهُ إِلَّا قَدْ حَبِطَ أَجْرُهُ قَالَ فَتَكَلَّمُوا فِي ذَلِكَ حَتَّى سَمِعَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَصْوَاتَهُمْ فَقَالَ بَلْ يُحْمَدُ وَيُؤْجَرُ قَالَ فَسُرَّ بِذَلِكَ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ حَتَّى هَمَّ أَنْ يَجْثُوَ عَلَى رُكْبَتَيْهِ فَقَالَ آنْتَ سَمِعْتَهُ مِرَارًا قَالَ نَعَمْ
matn_ar_clean: ول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم وقال يا فلان لو رأيت فلانا طعن ثم قال خذها وأنا الغلام الغفاري فما ترى قال ما أراه إلا قد حبط أجره قال فتكلموا في ذلك حتى سمع النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم أصواتهم فقال بل يحمد ويؤجر قال فسر بذلك أبو الدرداء حتى هم أن يجثو على ركبتيه فقال آنت سمعته مرارا قال نعم

isnad_chain.name_ar: أبيه وكان جليسا لأبي الدرداء بدمشق

iwh_en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Hishām ibn Saʿd narrated to us, Qays ibn Bishr al-Taghlibī narrated to me, from his father — who used to sit with Abū al-Dardāʾ in Damascus — who said: There was in Damascus a man called Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah, a solitary man who hardly spoke to anyone. He was always in prayer, and when he finished, he would glorify, magnify, and declare the oneness of Allah until he returned to his family. One day he passed by us while we were with Abū al-Dardāʾ. Abū al-Dardāʾ said to him, 'A word from you that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said, 'the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent us on a military detachment. When we returned, a man from among them sat in a gathering where the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was and said, "O so-and-so, if you had seen so-and-so thrust his spear and then say, 'Take that, and I am the Ghifārī lad!' — what do you think?" He said, "I think his reward has been nullified." They discussed this until the Prophet ﷺ heard their voices and said, "Rather, he is praised and rewarded."' Abū al-Dardāʾ was so delighted by this that he nearly knelt before him, saying, 'Did you hear that?' — repeating it several times, and he said, 'Yes.'

ar: ‏ ‏ثُمَّ مَرَّ عَلَيْنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏نِعْمَ الرَّجُلُ ‏ ‏خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ ‏ ‏لَوْ قَصَّ مِنْ شَعَرِهِ وَقَصَّرَ ‏ ‏إِزَارَهُ ‏ ‏فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ ‏ ‏خُرَيْمًا ‏ ‏فَعَجَّلَ ‏ ‏فَأَخَذَ ‏ ‏الشَّفْرَةَ ‏ ‏فَقَصَّرَ مِنْ ‏ ‏جُمَّتِهِ ‏ ‏وَرَفَعَ ‏ ‏إِزَارَهُ ‏ ‏إِلَى أَنْصَافِ سَاقَيْهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَى ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏فَرَأَيْتُ رَجُلًا مَعَهُ عَلَى السَّرِيرِ شَعَرُهُ فَوْقَ أُذُنَيْهِ ‏ ‏مُؤْتَزِرًا ‏ ‏إِلَى أَنْصَافِ سَاقَيْهِ قُلْتُ مَنْ هَذَا قَالُوا ‏ ‏خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ ‏
ar_clean: ثُمَّ مَرَّ عَلَيْنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقُولُ نِعْمَ الرَّجُلُ خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ لَوْ قَصَّ مِنْ شَعَرِهِ وَقَصَّرَ إِزَارَهُ فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ خُرَيْمًا فَعَجَّلَ فَأَخَذَ الشَّفْرَةَ فَقَصَّرَ مِنْ جُمَّتِهِ وَرَفَعَ إِزَارَهُ إِلَى أَنْصَافِ سَاقَيْهِ قَالَ أَبِي فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَى مُعَاوِيَةَ فَرَأَيْتُ رَجُلًا مَعَهُ عَلَى السَّرِيرِ شَعَرُهُ فَوْقَ أُذُنَيْهِ مُؤْتَزِرًا إِلَى أَنْصَافِ سَاقَيْهِ قُلْتُ مَنْ هَذَا قَالُوا خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ
sunnah_ref_en: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said, 'A word that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, 'What an excellent man Khuraym al-Asadī is, if only he would trim his hair and shorten his lower garment.' When this reached Khuraym, he hastily took a blade and trimmed his hair and raised his lower garment to mid-shin. My father said: I later visited Muʿāwiyah and saw a man with him on the couch, his hair above his ears, wearing his lower garment to mid-shin. I asked, 'Who is this?' They said, 'Khuraym al-Asadī.'
iwh_id: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ berkata, 'A word that benefits us and tidaklah harm you.' He berkata: aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ seraya berkata, 'What an excellent man Khuraym al-Asadī is, if only he would trim his hair and shorten his lower garment.' When this reached Khuraym, he hastily took a blade and trimmed his hair and raised his lower garment to mid-shin. My father berkata: I later visited Muʿāwiyah and saw a man with him on the couch, his hair above his ears, wearing his lower garment to mid-shin. aku bertanya, 'Who is this?' They berkata, 'Khuraym al-Asadī.'
isnad_ar: ثُمَّ مَرَّ عَلَيْنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ كَلِمَةً تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقُولُ نِعْمَ الرَّجُلُ خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ لَوْ قَصّ
matn_ar: َ مِنْ شَعَرِهِ وَقَصَّرَ إِزَارَهُ فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ خُرَيْمًا فَعَجَّلَ فَأَخَذَ الشَّفْرَةَ فَقَصَّرَ مِنْ جُمَّتِهِ وَرَفَعَ إِزَارَهُ إِلَى أَنْصَافِ سَاقَيْهِ قَالَ أَبِي فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَى مُعَاوِيَةَ فَرَأَيْتُ رَجُلًا مَعَهُ عَلَى السَّرِيرِ شَعَرُهُ فَوْقَ أُذُنَيْهِ مُؤْتَزِرًا إِلَى أَنْصَافِ سَاقَيْهِ قُلْتُ مَنْ هَذَا قَالُوا خُرَيْمٌ الْأَسَدِيُّ
matn_ar_clean: من شعره وقصر إزاره فبلغ ذلك خريما فعجل فأخذ الشفرة فقصر من جمته ورفع إزاره إلى أنصاف ساقيه قال أبي فدخلت على معاوية فرأيت رجلا معه على السرير شعره فوق أذنيه مؤتزرا إلى أنصاف ساقيه قلت من هذا قالوا خريم الأسدي
iwh_en: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said, 'A word that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, 'What an excellent man Khuraym al-Asadī is, if only he would trim his hair and shorten his lower garment.' When this reached Khuraym, he hastily took a blade and trimmed his hair and raised his lower garment to mid-shin. My father said: I later visited Muʿāwiyah and saw a man with him on the couch, his hair above his ears, wearing his lower garment to mid-shin. I asked, 'Who is this?' They said, 'Khuraym al-Asadī.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ مَرَّ عَلَيْنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏كَلِمَةً مِنْكَ تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ نَعَمْ كُنَّا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَنَا ‏ ‏إِنَّكُمْ قَادِمُونَ عَلَى إِخْوَانِكُمْ فَأَصْلِحُوا ‏ ‏رِحَالَكُمْ ‏ ‏وَلِبَاسَكُمْ حَتَّى تَكُونُوا فِي النَّاسِ كَأَنَّكُمْ ‏ ‏شَامَةٌ ‏ ‏فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لَا يُحِبُّ ‏ ‏الْفُحْشَ ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏التَّفَحُّشَ ‏
ar_clean: قَالَ ثُمَّ مَرَّ عَلَيْنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ كَلِمَةً مِنْكَ تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ نَعَمْ كُنَّا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ لَنَا إِنَّكُمْ قَادِمُونَ عَلَى إِخْوَانِكُمْ فَأَصْلِحُوا رِحَالَكُمْ وَلِبَاسَكُمْ حَتَّى تَكُونُوا فِي النَّاسِ كَأَنَّكُمْ شَامَةٌ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لَا يُحِبُّ الْفُحْشَ وَلَا التَّفَحُّشَ
sunnah_ref_en: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said, 'A word from you that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said, 'Yes. We were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he said to us, "You are coming to your brethren, so fix your mounts and your clothing until you are like a beauty mark among the people, for Allah عز وجل does not love obscenity or deliberate obscenity."'
iwh_id: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ berkata, 'A word from you that benefits us and does not harm you.' Dia berkata, 'Yes. We were with Rasulullah ﷺ and dia berkata to us, "You are coming to your brethren, so fix your mounts and your clothing until you are like a beauty mark among the people, for Allah عز وجل does not love obscenity or deliberate obscenity."'
matn_ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ مَرَّ عَلَيْنَا يَوْمًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ ‏ ‏كَلِمَةً مِنْكَ تَنْفَعُنَا وَلَا تَضُرُّكَ قَالَ نَعَمْ كُنَّا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَنَا ‏ ‏إِنَّكُمْ قَادِمُونَ عَلَى إِخْوَانِكُمْ فَأَصْلِحُوا ‏ ‏رِحَالَكُمْ ‏ ‏وَلِبَاسَكُمْ حَتَّى تَكُونُوا فِي النَّاسِ كَأَنَّكُمْ ‏ ‏شَامَةٌ ‏ ‏فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لَا يُحِبُّ ‏ ‏الْفُحْشَ ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏التَّفَحُّشَ ‏
matn_ar_clean: ‏ ‏قال ‏ ‏ثم مر علينا يوما آخر فقال ‏ ‏أبو الدرداء ‏ ‏كلمة منك تنفعنا ولا تضرك قال نعم كنا مع رسول الله ‏ ‏صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏ ‏فقال لنا ‏ ‏إنكم قادمون على إخوانكم فأصلحوا ‏ ‏رحالكم ‏ ‏ولباسكم حتى تكونوا في الناس كأنكم ‏ ‏شامة ‏ ‏فإن الله عز وجل لا يحب ‏ ‏الفحش ‏ ‏ولا ‏ ‏التفحش ‏
iwh_en: Then he passed by us another day, and Abū al-Dardāʾ said, 'A word from you that benefits us and does not harm you.' He said, 'Yes. We were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he said to us, "You are coming to your brethren, so fix your mounts and your clothing until you are like a beauty mark among the people, for Allah عز وجل does not love obscenity or deliberate obscenity."'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏رَبِيعَةُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو كَبْشَةَ السَّلُولِيُّ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ ‏ ‏سَهْلَ ابْنَ الْحَنْظَلِيَّةِ الْأَنْصَارِيَّ صَاحِبَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏عُيَيْنَةَ ‏ ‏والْأَقْرَعَ ‏ ‏سَأَلَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏شَيْئًا فَأَمَرَ ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏أَنْ يَكْتُبَ بِهِ لَهُمَا فَفَعَلَ وَخَتَمَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَمَرَ بِدَفْعِهِ ‏ ‏إِلَيْهِمَا فَأَمَّا ‏ ‏عُيَيْنَةُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ مَا فِيهِ قَالَ فِيهِ الَّذِي أُمِرْتُ بِهِ فَقَبَّلَهُ وَعَقَدَهُ فِي عِمَامَتِهِ وَكَانَ أَحْكَمَ الرَّجُلَيْنِ وَأَمَّا ‏ ‏الْأَقْرَعُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ أَحْمِلُ صَحِيفَةً لَا أَدْرِي مَا فِيهَا كَصَحِيفَةِ ‏ ‏الْمُتَلَمِّسِ ‏ ‏فَأَخْبَرَ ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ ‏ ‏رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِقَوْلِهِمَا وَخَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي حَاجَةٍ فَمَرَّ بِبَعِيرٍ ‏ ‏مُنَاخٍ ‏ ‏عَلَى بَابِ الْمَسْجِدِ مِنْ أَوَّلِ النَّهَارِ ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِهِ آخِرَ النَّهَارِ وَهُوَ عَلَى حَالِهِ فَقَالَ أَيْنَ صَاحِبُ هَذَا الْبَعِيرِ فَابْتُغِيَ فَلَمْ يُوجَدْ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ فِي هَذِهِ الْبَهَائِمِ ثُمَّ ارْكَبُوهَا صِحَاحًا وَارْكَبُوهَا سِمَانًا ‏ ‏كَالْمُتَسَخِّطِ ‏ ‏آنِفًا ‏ ‏إِنَّهُ مَنْ سَأَلَ وَعِنْدَهُ مَا يُغْنِيهِ فَإِنَّمَا يَسْتَكْثِرُ مِنْ نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ قَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَمَا يُغْنِيهِ قَالَ مَا ‏ ‏يُغَدِّيهِ أَوْ ‏ ‏يُعَشِّيهِ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ حَدَّثَنِي الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي رَبِيعَةُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو كَبْشَةَ السَّلُولِيُّ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ سَهْلَ ابْنَ الْحَنْظَلِيَّةِ الْأَنْصَارِيَّ صَاحِبَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنَّ عُيَيْنَةَ والْأَقْرَعَ سَأَلَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ شَيْئًا فَأَمَرَ مُعَاوِيَةَ أَنْ يَكْتُبَ بِهِ لَهُمَا فَفَعَلَ وَخَتَمَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَأَمَرَ بِدَفْعِهِ إِلَيْهِمَا فَأَمَّا عُيَيْنَةُ فَقَالَ مَا فِيهِ قَالَ فِيهِ الَّذِي أُمِرْتُ بِهِ فَقَبَّلَهُ وَعَقَدَهُ فِي عِمَامَتِهِ وَكَانَ أَحْكَمَ الرَّجُلَيْنِ وَأَمَّا الْأَقْرَعُ فَقَالَ أَحْمِلُ صَحِيفَةً لَا أَدْرِي مَا فِيهَا كَصَحِيفَةِ الْمُتَلَمِّسِ فَأَخْبَرَ مُعَاوِيَةُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِقَوْلِهِمَا وَخَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي حَاجَةٍ فَمَرَّ بِبَعِيرٍ مُنَاخٍ عَلَى بَابِ الْمَسْجِدِ مِنْ أَوَّلِ النَّهَارِ ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِهِ آخِرَ النَّهَارِ وَهُوَ عَلَى حَالِهِ فَقَالَ أَيْنَ صَاحِبُ هَذَا الْبَعِيرِ فَابْتُغِيَ فَلَمْ يُوجَدْ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ فِي هَذِهِ الْبَهَائِمِ ثُمَّ ارْكَبُوهَا صِحَاحًا وَارْكَبُوهَا سِمَانًا كَالْمُتَسَخِّطِ آنِفًا إِنَّهُ مَنْ سَأَلَ وَعِنْدَهُ مَا يُغْنِيهِ فَإِنَّمَا يَسْتَكْثِرُ مِنْ نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ قَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَمَا يُغْنِيهِ قَالَ مَا يُغَدِّيهِ أَوْ يُعَشِّيهِ
sunnah_ref_en: ʿAlī ibn ʿAbdullāh narrated to us, al-Walīd ibn Muslim narrated to me, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd ibn Jābir narrated to me saying: Rabīʿah ibn Yazīd narrated to me, Abū Kabshah al-Salūlī narrated to me that he heard Sahl Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah al-Anṣārī, the Companion of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, [say] that ʿUyaynah and al-Aqraʿ asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ for something, and he ordered Muʿāwiyah to write it for them. He did so, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sealed it and ordered it given to them. As for ʿUyaynah, he said, 'What is in it?' He was told, 'In it is what you were commanded.' So he accepted it and tied it in his turban — and he was the wiser of the two men. As for al-Aqraʿ, he said, 'Shall I carry a letter not knowing what is in it, like the letter of al-Mutalammis?' So Muʿāwiyah informed the Messenger of Allah ﷺ of their statements. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out for some need and passed by a camel kneeling at the door of the mosque from the beginning of the day. He passed by it again at the end of the day and it was still in the same state. He said, 'Where is the owner of this camel?' He was sought but not found. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'Fear Allah regarding these animals. Ride them when they are healthy and ride them when they are well-fed.' Then, as if displeased, he said, 'Whoever asks while he has what suffices him is only accumulating from the Fire of Hell.' They said, 'O Messenger of Allah, what suffices him?' He said, 'What provides him lunch or dinner.'
iwh_id: ʿAlī ibn ʿAbdullāh menceritakan kepada kami, al-Walīd ibn Muslim menceritakan kepadaku, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd ibn Jābir menceritakan kepadaku seraya berkata: Rabīʿah ibn Yazīd menceritakan kepadaku, Abū Kabshah al-Salūlī menceritakan kepadaku that dia mendengar Sahl Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah al-Anṣārī, the Companion of Rasulullah ﷺ, [say] that ʿUyaynah and al-Aqraʿ asked Rasulullah ﷺ for something, and he ordered Muʿāwiyah to write it for them. He did so, and Rasulullah ﷺ sealed it and ordered it given to them. As for ʿUyaynah, dia berkata, 'What is in it?' He was told, 'In it is what you were commanded.' So he accepted it and tied it in his turban — and he was the wiser of the two men. As for al-Aqraʿ, dia berkata, 'Shall I carry a letter not knowing what is in it, like the letter of al-Mutalammis?' So Muʿāwiyah informed Rasulullah ﷺ of their statements. Then Rasulullah ﷺ went out for some need and passed by a camel kneeling at the door of the mosque dari the beginning of the day. He passed by it again at the end of the day and it was still in the same state. He berkata, 'Where is the owner of this camel?' He was sought but not found. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, 'Fear Allah regarding these animals. Ride them when they are healthy and ride them when they are well-fed.' Then, as if displeased, dia berkata, 'Whoever asks while he has what suffices him is only accumulating from neraka of neraka.' They berkata, 'O Messenger of Allah, what suffices him?' He berkata, 'What provides him lunch or dinner.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ حَدَّثَنِي الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي رَبِيعَةُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو كَبْشَةَ السَّلُولِيُّ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ سَهْلَ ابْنَ الْحَنْظَلِيَّةِ الْأَنْصَارِيَّ صَاحِبَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنَّ عُيَيْنَةَ والْأَقْرَعَ سَأَلَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ شَيْئًا فَأَمَرَ مُعَاوِيَةَ أَنْ يَكْتُبَ بِهِ لَهُمَا فَفَعَلَ وَخَتَمَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَأَمَرَ بِدَفْعِهِ إِلَيْهِمَا فَأَمَّا عُيَيْنَةُ فَقَالَ مَا فِيهِ قَالَ فِيهِ الَّذِي أُمِرْتُ بِهِ فَقَبَّلَهُ وَعَقَدَهُ فِي عِمَامَتِهِ وَكَانَ أَحْكَمَ الرَّجُلَيْنِ وَأَمَّا الْأَقْرَعُ فَقَالَ أَحْمِلُ صَحِيفَةً لَا أَدْرِي مَا فِيهَا كَصَحِيفَةِ الْمُتَلَمِّسِ فَأَخْبَرَ مُعَاوِيَةُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِ
matn_ar: قَوْلِهِمَا وَخَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي حَاجَةٍ فَمَرَّ بِبَعِيرٍ مُنَاخٍ عَلَى بَابِ الْمَسْجِدِ مِنْ أَوَّلِ النَّهَارِ ثُمَّ مَرَّ بِهِ آخِرَ النَّهَارِ وَهُوَ عَلَى حَالِهِ فَقَالَ أَيْنَ صَاحِبُ هَذَا الْبَعِيرِ فَابْتُغِيَ فَلَمْ يُوجَدْ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ فِي هَذِهِ الْبَهَائِمِ ثُمَّ ارْكَبُوهَا صِحَاحًا وَارْكَبُوهَا سِمَانًا كَالْمُتَسَخِّطِ آنِفًا إِنَّهُ مَنْ سَأَلَ وَعِنْدَهُ مَا يُغْنِيهِ فَإِنَّمَا يَسْتَكْثِرُ مِنْ نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ قَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَمَا يُغْنِيهِ قَالَ مَا يُغَدِّيهِ أَوْ يُعَشِّيهِ
matn_ar_clean: قولهما وخرج رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم في حاجة فمر ببعير مناخ على باب المسجد من أول النهار ثم مر به آخر النهار وهو على حاله فقال أين صاحب هذا البعير فابتغي فلم يوجد فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم اتقوا الله في هذه البهائم ثم اركبوها صحاحا واركبوها سمانا كالمتسخط آنفا إنه من سأل وعنده ما يغنيه فإنما يستكثر من نار جهنم قالوا يا رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم وما يغنيه قال ما يغديه أو يعشيه
iwh_en: ʿAlī ibn ʿAbdullāh narrated to us, al-Walīd ibn Muslim narrated to me, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd ibn Jābir narrated to me saying: Rabīʿah ibn Yazīd narrated to me, Abū Kabshah al-Salūlī narrated to me that he heard Sahl Ibn al-Ḥanẓaliyyah al-Anṣārī, the Companion of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, [say] that ʿUyaynah and al-Aqraʿ asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ for something, and he ordered Muʿāwiyah to write it for them. He did so, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sealed it and ordered it given to them. As for ʿUyaynah, he said, 'What is in it?' He was told, 'In it is what you were commanded.' So he accepted it and tied it in his turban — and he was the wiser of the two men. As for al-Aqraʿ, he said, 'Shall I carry a letter not knowing what is in it, like the letter of al-Mutalammis?' So Muʿāwiyah informed the Messenger of Allah ﷺ of their statements. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out for some need and passed by a camel kneeling at the door of the mosque from the beginning of the day. He passed by it again at the end of the day and it was still in the same state. He said, 'Where is the owner of this camel?' He was sought but not found. the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'Fear Allah regarding these animals. Ride them when they are healthy and ride them when they are well-fed.' Then, as if displeased, he said, 'Whoever asks while he has what suffices him is only accumulating from the Fire of Hell.' They said, 'O Messenger of Allah, what suffices him?' He said, 'What provides him lunch or dinner.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَى ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَيَّاشُ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شِيَيْمِ بْنِ بَيْتَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جُنَادَةَ بْنِ أَبِي أُمَيَّةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ ‏ ‏بِرُودِسَ ‏ ‏حِينَ جَلَدَ الرَّجُلَيْنِ اللَّذَيْنِ سَرَقَا غَنَائِمَ النَّاسِ فَقَالَ إِنَّهُ لَمْ يَمْنَعْنِي مِنْ قَطْعِهِمَا إِلَّا أَنَّ ‏ ‏بُسْرَ بْنَ أَرْطَأَةَ ‏ ‏وَجَدَ رَجُلًا سَرَقَ فِي الْغَزْوِ يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏مَصْدَرٌ ‏ ‏فَجَلَدَهُ وَلَمْ يَقْطَعْ يَدَهُ وَقَالَ ‏ ‏نَهَانَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ الْقَطْعِ فِي الْغَزْوِ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا حَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَى حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ لَهِيعَةَ حَدَّثَنَا عَيَّاشُ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عَنْ شِيَيْمِ بْنِ بَيْتَانَ عَنْ جُنَادَةَ بْنِ أَبِي أُمَيَّةَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ بِرُودِسَ حِينَ جَلَدَ الرَّجُلَيْنِ اللَّذَيْنِ سَرَقَا غَنَائِمَ النَّاسِ فَقَالَ إِنَّهُ لَمْ يَمْنَعْنِي مِنْ قَطْعِهِمَا إِلَّا أَنَّ بُسْرَ بْنَ أَرْطَأَةَ وَجَدَ رَجُلًا سَرَقَ فِي الْغَزْوِ يُقَالُ لَهُ مَصْدَرٌ فَجَلَدَهُ وَلَمْ يَقْطَعْ يَدَهُ وَقَالَ نَهَانَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنْ الْقَطْعِ فِي الْغَزْوِ
sunnah_ref_en: Ḥasan ibn Mūsā narrated to us, ʿAbdullāh ibn Lahīʿah narrated to us, ʿAyyāsh ibn ʿAbbās narrated to us, from Shiyaym ibn Baytān, from Junādah ibn Abī Umayyah: that he said on the pulpit at Rhodes when he flogged the two men who stole from the people's war spoils: 'Nothing prevented me from cutting them except that Busr ibn Arṭāʾah found a man who stole during a military expedition — called Maṣdar — and he flogged him but did not cut his hand, and said, "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade us from cutting [hands] during military expeditions."'
iwh_id: Ḥasan ibn Mūsā menceritakan kepada kami, ʿAbdullāh ibn Lahīʿah menceritakan kepada kami, ʿAyyāsh ibn ʿAbbās menceritakan kepada kami, dari Shiyaym ibn Baytān, dari Junādah ibn Abī Umayyah: that dia berkata on the pulpit at Rhodes when he flogged the two men who stole dari the people's war spoils: 'Nothing prevented me from cutting them except that Busr ibn Arṭāʾah found a man who stole during a military expedition — called Maṣdar — and he flogged him but did not cut his hand, and berkata, "Rasulullah ﷺ melarang us from cutting [hands] during military expeditions."'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا حَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَى حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ لَهِيعَةَ حَدَّثَنَا عَيَّاشُ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عَنْ شِيَيْمِ بْنِ بَيْتَانَ عَنْ جُنَادَةَ بْنِ أَبِي أُمَيَّةَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ بِرُودِسَ حِينَ جَلَدَ الرَّجُلَيْنِ اللَّذَيْنِ سَرَقَا غَنَائِمَ
matn_ar: النَّاسِ فَقَالَ إِنَّهُ لَمْ يَمْنَعْنِي مِنْ قَطْعِهِمَا إِلَّا أَنَّ بُسْرَ بْنَ أَرْطَأَةَ وَجَدَ رَجُلًا سَرَقَ فِي الْغَزْوِ يُقَالُ لَهُ مَصْدَرٌ فَجَلَدَهُ وَلَمْ يَقْطَعْ يَدَهُ وَقَالَ نَهَانَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنْ الْقَطْعِ فِي الْغَزْوِ
matn_ar_clean: الناس فقال إنه لم يمنعني من قطعهما إلا أن بسر بن أرطأة وجد رجلا سرق في الغزو يقال له مصدر فجلده ولم يقطع يده وقال نهانا رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم عن القطع في الغزو
iwh_en: Ḥasan ibn Mūsā narrated to us, ʿAbdullāh ibn Lahīʿah narrated to us, ʿAyyāsh ibn ʿAbbās narrated to us, from Shiyaym ibn Baytān, from Junādah ibn Abī Umayyah: that he said on the pulpit at Rhodes when he flogged the two men who stole from the people's war spoils: 'Nothing prevented me from cutting them except that Busr ibn Arṭāʾah found a man who stole during a military expedition — called Maṣdar — and he flogged him but did not cut his hand, and said, "the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade us from cutting [hands] during military expeditions."'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَتَّابُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏سَعِيدُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَيَّاشُ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شِيَيْمِ بْنِ بَيْتَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جُنَادَةَ بْنِ أَبِي أُمَيَّةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏بُسْرِ بْنِ أَرْطَأَةَ ‏ ‏فَأُتِيَ ‏ ‏بِمَصْدَرٍ ‏ ‏قَدْ سَرَقَ ‏ ‏بُخْتِيَّةً ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَوْلَا أَنِّي سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نَهَانَا عَنْ الْقَطْعِ فِي الْغَزْوِ لَقَطَعْتُكَ فَجُلِدَ ثُمَّ خُلِّيَ سَبِيلُهُ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا عَتَّابُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَيَّاشُ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عَنْ شِيَيْمِ بْنِ بَيْتَانَ عَنْ جُنَادَةَ بْنِ أَبِي أُمَيَّةَ قَالَ كُنْتُ عِنْدَ بُسْرِ بْنِ أَرْطَأَةَ فَأُتِيَ بِمَصْدَرٍ قَدْ سَرَقَ بُخْتِيَّةً فَقَالَ لَوْلَا أَنِّي سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَهَانَا عَنْ الْقَطْعِ فِي الْغَزْوِ لَقَطَعْتُكَ فَجُلِدَ ثُمَّ خُلِّيَ سَبِيلُهُ
sunnah_ref_en: ʿAttāb ibn Ziyād narrated to us saying: ʿAbdullāh informed us saying: Saʿīd ibn Yazīd informed us saying: ʿAyyāsh ibn ʿAbbās narrated to us, from Shiyaym ibn Baytān, from Junādah ibn Abī Umayyah who said: I was with Busr ibn Arṭāʾah when a man named Maṣdar who had stolen a Bactrian camel was brought to him. He said, 'Were it not that I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbid us from cutting [hands] during military expeditions, I would have cut yours.' So he was flogged and released.
iwh_id: ʿAttāb ibn Ziyād menceritakan kepada kami seraya berkata: ʿAbdullāh mengabarkan kepada kami seraya berkata: Saʿīd ibn Yazīd mengabarkan kepada kami seraya berkata: ʿAyyāsh ibn ʿAbbās menceritakan kepada kami, dari Shiyaym ibn Baytān, dari Junādah ibn Abī Umayyah yang berkata: I was with Busr ibn Arṭāʾah when a man named Maṣdar who had stolen a Bactrian camel was brought to him. He berkata, 'Were it not that aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ melarang us from cutting [hands] during military expeditions, I would have cut yours.' So he was flogged and released.
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا عَتَّابُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَيَّاشُ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عَنْ شِيَيْمِ بْنِ بَيْتَانَ
matn_ar: عَنْ جُنَادَةَ بْنِ أَبِي أُمَيَّةَ قَالَ كُنْتُ عِنْدَ بُسْرِ بْنِ أَرْطَأَةَ فَأُتِيَ بِمَصْدَرٍ قَدْ سَرَقَ بُخْتِيَّةً فَقَالَ لَوْلَا أَنِّي سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَهَانَا عَنْ الْقَطْعِ فِي الْغَزْوِ لَقَطَعْتُكَ فَجُلِدَ ثُمَّ خُلِّيَ سَبِيلُهُ
matn_ar_clean: عن جنادة بن أبي أمية قال كنت عند بسر بن أرطأة فأتي بمصدر قد سرق بختية فقال لولا أني سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم نهانا عن القطع في الغزو لقطعتك فجلد ثم خلي سبيله
iwh_en: ʿAttāb ibn Ziyād narrated to us saying: ʿAbdullāh informed us saying: Saʿīd ibn Yazīd informed us saying: ʿAyyāsh ibn ʿAbbās narrated to us, from Shiyaym ibn Baytān, from Junādah ibn Abī Umayyah who said: I was with Busr ibn Arṭāʾah when a man named Maṣdar who had stolen a Bactrian camel was brought to him. He said, 'Were it not that I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbid us from cutting [hands] during military expeditions, I would have cut yours.' So he was flogged and released.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَيْثَمُ بْنُ خَارِجَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَيُّوبَ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ بْنِ حَلْبَسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏بُسْرِ بْنِ أَرْطَاةَ الْقُرَشِيِّ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَدْعُو ‏ ‏اللَّهُمَّ أَحْسِنْ عَاقِبَتَنَا فِي الْأُمُورِ كُلِّهَا وَأَجِرْنَا مِنْ خِزْيِ الدُّنْيَا وَعَذَابِ الْآخِرَةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏وَسَمِعْتُهُ ‏ ‏أَنَا مِنْ ‏ ‏هَيْثَمٍ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا هَيْثَمُ بْنُ خَارِجَةَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَيُّوبَ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ بْنِ حَلْبَسٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبِي يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ بُسْرِ بْنِ أَرْطَاةَ الْقُرَشِيِّ يَقُولُ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَدْعُو اللَّهُمَّ أَحْسِنْ عَاقِبَتَنَا فِي الْأُمُورِ كُلِّهَا وَأَجِرْنَا مِنْ خِزْيِ الدُّنْيَا وَعَذَابِ الْآخِرَةِ قَالَ عَبْد اللَّهِ وَسَمِعْتُهُ أَنَا مِنْ هَيْثَمٍ
sunnah_ref_en: Haytham ibn Khārijah narrated to us, Muḥammad ibn Ayyūb ibn Maysarah ibn Ḥalbas narrated to us saying: I heard my father narrating from Busr ibn Arṭāʾah al-Qurashī who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ supplicating, 'O Allah, make good our end in all matters and protect us from the disgrace of this world and the punishment of the Hereafter.' ʿAbdullāh said: I too heard it from Haytham.
iwh_id: Haytham ibn Khārijah menceritakan kepada kami, Muḥammad ibn Ayyūb ibn Maysarah ibn Ḥalbas menceritakan kepada kami seraya berkata: aku mendengar my father narrating dari Busr ibn Arṭāʾah al-Qurashī yang berkata: aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ supplicating, 'O Allah, make good our end in all matters and protect us dari the disgrace of this world and the punishment of the akhirat.' ʿAbdullāh berkata: I too heard it dari Haytham.
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا هَيْثَمُ بْنُ خَارِجَةَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَيُّوبَ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ بْنِ حَلْبَسٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبِي يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ بُسْرِ بْنِ أَرْطَاةَ الْقُرَشِيِّ يَقُولُ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّ
matn_ar: ى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَدْعُو اللَّهُمَّ أَحْسِنْ عَاقِبَتَنَا فِي الْأُمُورِ كُلِّهَا وَأَجِرْنَا مِنْ خِزْيِ الدُّنْيَا وَعَذَابِ الْآخِرَةِ قَالَ عَبْد اللَّهِ وَسَمِعْتُهُ أَنَا مِنْ هَيْثَمٍ
matn_ar_clean: ى الله عليه وسلم يدعو اللهم أحسن عاقبتنا في الأمور كلها وأجرنا من خزي الدنيا وعذاب الآخرة قال عبد الله وسمعته أنا من هيثم
iwh_en: Haytham ibn Khārijah narrated to us, Muḥammad ibn Ayyūb ibn Maysarah ibn Ḥalbas narrated to us saying: I heard my father narrating from Busr ibn Arṭāʾah al-Qurashī who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ supplicating, 'O Allah, make good our end in all matters and protect us from the disgrace of this world and the punishment of the Hereafter.' ʿAbdullāh said: I too heard it from Haytham.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبُو الْعَبَّاسِ الدِّمَشْقِيُّ ‏ ‏بِمَكَّةَ ‏ ‏إِمْلَاءً ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ جَابِرٍ الطَّائِيُّ ‏ ‏قَاضِي ‏ ‏حِمْصَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ جُبَيْرِ بْنِ نُفَيْرٍ الْحَضْرَمِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ ‏ ‏النَّوَّاسَ بْنَ سَمْعَانَ الْكِلَابِيَّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ذَكَرَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الدَّجَّالَ ‏ ‏ذَاتَ ‏ ‏غَدَاةٍ ‏ ‏فَخَفَّضَ فِيهِ وَرَفَّعَ حَتَّى ظَنَنَّاهُ فِي طَائِفَةِ النَّخْلِ فَلَمَّا رُحْنَا إِلَيْهِ عَرَفَ ذَلِكَ فِي وُجُوهِنَا فَسَأَلْنَاهُ فَقُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ذَكَرْتَ ‏ ‏الدَّجَّالَ ‏ ‏الْغَدَاةَ ‏ ‏فَخَفَّضْتَ فِيهِ وَرَفَّعْتَ حَتَّى ظَنَنَّاهُ فِي طَائِفَةِ النَّخْلِ قَالَ ‏ ‏غَيْرُ ‏ ‏الدَّجَّالِ ‏ ‏أَخْوَفُنِي عَلَيْكُمْ فَإِنْ يَخْرُجْ وَأَنَا فِيكُمْ فَأَنَا ‏ ‏حَجِيجُهُ ‏ ‏دُونَكُمْ وَإِنْ يَخْرُجْ وَلَسْتُ فِيكُمْ فَامْرُؤٌ ‏ ‏حَجِيجُ ‏ ‏نَفْسِهِ وَاللَّهُ خَلِيفَتِي عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ إِنَّهُ شَابٌّ ‏ ‏جَعْدٌ ‏ ‏قَطَطٌ ‏ ‏عَيْنُهُ ‏ ‏طَافِيَةٌ ‏ ‏وَإِنَّهُ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ ‏ ‏خِلَّةٍ ‏ ‏بَيْنَ ‏ ‏الشَّامِ ‏ ‏وَالْعِرَاقِ ‏ ‏فَعَاثَ ‏ ‏يَمِينًا وَشِمَالًا يَا عِبَادَ اللَّهِ اثْبُتُوا قُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ مَا ‏ ‏لُبْثُهُ ‏ ‏فِي الْأَرْضِ قَالَ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمًا يَوْمٌ كَسَنَةٍ وَيَوْمٌ كَشَهْرٍ وَيَوْمٌ كَجُمُعَةٍ وَسَائِرُ أَيَّامِهِ كَأَيَّامِكُمْ قُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَذَلِكَ الْيَوْمُ الَّذِي هُوَ كَسَنَةٍ ‏ ‏أَيَكْفِينَا فِيهِ صَلَاةُ يَوْمٍ وَلَيْلَةٍ قَالَ لَا اقْدُرُوا لَهُ قَدْرَهُ قُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَمَا إِسْرَاعُهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ قَالَ ‏ ‏كَالْغَيْثِ ‏ ‏اسْتَدْبَرَتْهُ ‏ ‏الرِّيحُ قَالَ فَيَمُرُّ بِالْحَيِّ فَيَدْعُوهُمْ فَيَسْتَجِيبُونَ لَهُ فَيَأْمُرُ السَّمَاءَ فَتُمْطِرُ وَالْأَرْضَ فَتُنْبِتُ ‏ ‏وَتَرُوحُ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِمْ ‏ ‏سَارِحَتُهُمْ ‏ ‏وَهِيَ أَطْوَلُ مَا كَانَتْ ‏ ‏ذُرًى ‏ ‏وَأَمَدُّهُ ‏ ‏خَوَاصِرَ ‏ ‏وَأَسْبَغُهُ ‏ ‏ضُرُوعًا وَيَمُرُّ بِالْحَيِّ فَيَدْعُوهُمْ فَيَرُدُّوا عَلَيْهِ قَوْلَهُ فَتَتْبَعُهُ أَمْوَالُهُمْ فَيُصْبِحُونَ ‏ ‏مُمْحِلِينَ ‏ ‏لَيْسَ لَهُمْ مِنْ أَمْوَالِهِمْ شَيْءٌ وَيَمُرُّ بِالْخَرِبَةِ فَيَقُولُ لَهَا أَخْرِجِي كُنُوزَكِ فَتَتْبَعُهُ كُنُوزُهَا ‏ ‏كَيَعَاسِيبِ ‏ ‏النَّحْلِ قَالَ وَيَأْمُرُ بِرَجُلٍ فَيُقْتَلُ فَيَضْرِبُهُ ‏ ‏بِالسَّيْفِ فَيَقْطَعُهُ ‏ ‏جِزْلَتَيْنِ ‏ ‏رَمْيَةَ ‏ ‏الْغَرَضِ ثُمَّ يَدْعُوهُ فَيُقْبِلُ إِلَيْهِ ‏ ‏يَتَهَلَّلُ ‏ ‏وَجْهُهُ قَالَ فَبَيْنَا هُوَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ إِذْ بَعَثَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ ‏الْمَسِيحَ ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام ‏ ‏فَيَنْزِلُ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏الْمَنَارَةِ الْبَيْضَاءِ ‏ ‏شَرْقِيَّ ‏ ‏دِمَشْقَ ‏ ‏بَيْنَ ‏ ‏مَهْرُودَتَيْنِ ‏ ‏وَاضِعًا يَدَهُ عَلَى أَجْنِحَةِ مَلَكَيْنِ فَيَتْبَعُهُ فَيُدْرِكُهُ فَيَقْتُلُهُ عِنْدَ بَابِ ‏ ‏لُدٍّ ‏ ‏الشَّرْقِيِّ قَالَ فَبَيْنَمَا هُمْ كَذَلِكَ إِذْ أَوْحَى اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ إِلَى ‏ ‏عِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام ‏ ‏أَنِّي قَدْ أَخْرَجْتُ عِبَادًا مِنْ عِبَادِي لَا ‏ ‏يَدَانِ ‏ ‏لَكَ بِقِتَالِهِمْ فَحَوِّزْ عِبَادِي إِلَى ‏ ‏الطُّورِ ‏ ‏فَيَبْعَثُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ ‏يَأْجُوجَ ‏ ‏وَمَأْجُوجَ ‏ ‏وَهُمْ كَمَا قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ { ‏مِنْ كُلِّ ‏ ‏حَدَبٍ ‏ ‏يَنْسِلُونَ ‏ } ‏فَيَرْغَبُ ‏ ‏عِيسَى ‏ ‏وَأَصْحَابُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَيُرْسِلُ عَلَيْهِمْ ‏ ‏نَغَفًا ‏ ‏فِي رِقَابِهِمْ فَيُصْبِحُونَ فَرْسَى كَمَوْتِ نَفْسٍ وَاحِدَةٍ فَيَهْبِطُ ‏ ‏عِيسَى ‏ ‏وَأَصْحَابُهُ فَلَا يَجِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بَيْتًا إِلَّا قَدْ مَلَأَهُ ‏ ‏زَهَمُهُمْ ‏ ‏وَنَتِنُهُمْ ‏ ‏فَيَرْغَبُ ‏ ‏عِيسَى ‏ ‏وَأَصْحَابُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَيُرْسِلُ عَلَيْهِمْ طَيْرًا كَأَعْنَاقِ ‏ ‏الْبُخْتِ ‏ ‏فَتَحْمِلُهُمْ ‏ ‏فَتَطْرَحُهُمْ ‏ ‏حَيْثُ شَاءَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏فَحَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَطَاءُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ السَّكْسَكِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏كَعْبٍ ‏ ‏أَوْ غَيْرِهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فَتَطْرَحُهُمْ ‏ ‏بِالْمُهَبَّلِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا ‏ ‏أَبَا يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏وَأَيْنَ الْمُهَبَّلُ قَالَ مَطْلَعُ الشَّمْسِ ‏ ‏قَالَ وَيُرْسِلُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ مَطَرًا لَا يَكُنْ مِنْهُ بَيْتُ ‏ ‏وَبَرٍ ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏مَدَرٍ ‏ ‏أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمًا فَيَغْسِلُ الْأَرْضَ حَتَّى يَتْرُكَهَا ‏ ‏كَالزَّلَفَةِ ‏ ‏وَيُقَالُ لِلْأَرْضِ أَنْبِتِي ثَمَرَتَكِ وَرُدِّي بَرَكَتَكِ قَالَ فَيَوْمَئِذٍ يَأْكُلُ ‏ ‏النَّفَرُ ‏ ‏مِنْ الرُّمَّانَةِ وَيَسْتَظِلُّونَ ‏ ‏بِقِحْفِهَا ‏ ‏وَيُبَارَكُ فِي الرِّسْلِ حَتَّى أَنَّ ‏ ‏اللَّقْحَةَ ‏ ‏مِنْ الْإِبِلِ ‏ ‏لَتَكْفِي ‏ ‏الْفِئَامَ ‏ ‏مِنْ النَّاسِ ‏ ‏وَاللَّقْحَةَ ‏ ‏مِنْ الْبَقَرِ ‏ ‏تَكْفِي ‏ ‏الْفَخِذَ ‏ ‏وَالشَّاةَ مِنْ الْغَنَمِ ‏ ‏تَكْفِي أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ قَالَ فَبَيْنَا هُمْ عَلَى ذَلِكَ إِذْ بَعَثَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ رِيحًا طَيِّبَةً تَحْتَ آبَاطِهِمْ فَتَقْبِضُ رُوحَ كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏أَوْ قَالَ كُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ ‏ ‏وَيَبْقَى شِرَارُ النَّاسِ ‏ ‏يَتَهَارَجُونَ ‏ ‏تَهَارُجَ ‏ ‏الْحَمِيرِ وَعَلَيْهِمْ ‏ ‏أَوْ قَالَ وَعَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبُو الْعَبَّاسِ الدِّمَشْقِيُّ بِمَكَّةَ إِمْلَاءً قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي يَحْيَى بْنُ جَابِرٍ الطَّائِيُّ قَاضِي حِمْصَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ جُبَيْرِ بْنِ نُفَيْرٍ الْحَضْرَمِيُّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ النَّوَّاسَ بْنَ سَمْعَانَ الْكِلَابِيَّ قَالَ ذَكَرَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ الدَّجَّالَ ذَاتَ غَدَاةٍ فَخَفَّضَ فِيهِ وَرَفَّعَ حَتَّى ظَنَنَّاهُ فِي طَائِفَةِ النَّخْلِ فَلَمَّا رُحْنَا إِلَيْهِ عَرَفَ ذَلِكَ فِي وُجُوهِنَا فَسَأَلْنَاهُ فَقُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ذَكَرْتَ الدَّجَّالَ الْغَدَاةَ فَخَفَّضْتَ فِيهِ وَرَفَّعْتَ حَتَّى ظَنَنَّاهُ فِي طَائِفَةِ النَّخْلِ قَالَ غَيْرُ الدَّجَّالِ أَخْوَفُنِي عَلَيْكُمْ فَإِنْ يَخْرُجْ وَأَنَا فِيكُمْ فَأَنَا حَجِيجُهُ دُونَكُمْ وَإِنْ يَخْرُجْ وَلَسْتُ فِيكُمْ فَامْرُؤٌ حَجِيجُ نَفْسِهِ وَاللَّهُ خَلِيفَتِي عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ إِنَّهُ شَابٌّ جَعْدٌ قَطَطٌ عَيْنُهُ طَافِيَةٌ وَإِنَّهُ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَّةٍ بَيْنَ الشَّامِ وَالْعِرَاقِ فَعَاثَ يَمِينًا وَشِمَالًا يَا عِبَادَ اللَّهِ اثْبُتُوا قُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ مَا لُبْثُهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ قَالَ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمًا يَوْمٌ كَسَنَةٍ وَيَوْمٌ كَشَهْرٍ وَيَوْمٌ كَجُمُعَةٍ وَسَائِرُ أَيَّامِهِ كَأَيَّامِكُمْ قُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَذَلِكَ الْيَوْمُ الَّذِي هُوَ كَسَنَةٍ أَيَكْفِينَا فِيهِ صَلَاةُ يَوْمٍ وَلَيْلَةٍ قَالَ لَا اقْدُرُوا لَهُ قَدْرَهُ قُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَمَا إِسْرَاعُهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ قَالَ كَالْغَيْثِ اسْتَدْبَرَتْهُ الرِّيحُ قَالَ فَيَمُرُّ بِالْحَيِّ فَيَدْعُوهُمْ فَيَسْتَجِيبُونَ لَهُ فَيَأْمُرُ السَّمَاءَ فَتُمْطِرُ وَالْأَرْضَ فَتُنْبِتُ وَتَرُوحُ عَلَيْهِمْ سَارِحَتُهُمْ وَهِيَ أَطْوَلُ مَا كَانَتْ ذُرًى وَأَمَدُّهُ خَوَاصِرَ وَأَسْبَغُهُ ضُرُوعًا وَيَمُرُّ بِالْحَيِّ فَيَدْعُوهُمْ فَيَرُدُّوا عَلَيْهِ قَوْلَهُ فَتَتْبَعُهُ أَمْوَالُهُمْ فَيُصْبِحُونَ مُمْحِلِينَ لَيْسَ لَهُمْ مِنْ أَمْوَالِهِمْ شَيْءٌ وَيَمُرُّ بِالْخَرِبَةِ فَيَقُولُ لَهَا أَخْرِجِي كُنُوزَكِ فَتَتْبَعُهُ كُنُوزُهَا كَيَعَاسِيبِ النَّحْلِ قَالَ وَيَأْمُرُ بِرَجُلٍ فَيُقْتَلُ فَيَضْرِبُهُ بِالسَّيْفِ فَيَقْطَعُهُ جِزْلَتَيْنِ رَمْيَةَ الْغَرَضِ ثُمَّ يَدْعُوهُ فَيُقْبِلُ إِلَيْهِ يَتَهَلَّلُ وَجْهُهُ قَالَ فَبَيْنَا هُوَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ إِذْ بَعَثَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ الْمَسِيحَ ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام فَيَنْزِلُ عِنْدَ الْمَنَارَةِ الْبَيْضَاءِ شَرْقِيَّ دِمَشْقَ بَيْنَ مَهْرُودَتَيْنِ وَاضِعًا يَدَهُ عَلَى أَجْنِحَةِ مَلَكَيْنِ فَيَتْبَعُهُ فَيُدْرِكُهُ فَيَقْتُلُهُ عِنْدَ بَابِ لُدٍّ الشَّرْقِيِّ قَالَ فَبَيْنَمَا هُمْ كَذَلِكَ إِذْ أَوْحَى اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ إِلَى عِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام أَنِّي قَدْ أَخْرَجْتُ عِبَادًا مِنْ عِبَادِي لَا يَدَانِ لَكَ بِقِتَالِهِمْ فَحَوِّزْ عِبَادِي إِلَى الطُّورِ فَيَبْعَثُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ وَهُمْ كَمَا قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ { مِنْ كُلِّ حَدَبٍ يَنْسِلُونَ } فَيَرْغَبُ عِيسَى وَأَصْحَابُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَيُرْسِلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَغَفًا فِي رِقَابِهِمْ فَيُصْبِحُونَ فَرْسَى كَمَوْتِ نَفْسٍ وَاحِدَةٍ فَيَهْبِطُ عِيسَى وَأَصْحَابُهُ فَلَا يَجِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بَيْتًا إِلَّا قَدْ مَلَأَهُ زَهَمُهُمْ وَنَتِنُهُمْ فَيَرْغَبُ عِيسَى وَأَصْحَابُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَيُرْسِلُ عَلَيْهِمْ طَيْرًا كَأَعْنَاقِ الْبُخْتِ فَتَحْمِلُهُمْ فَتَطْرَحُهُمْ حَيْثُ شَاءَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَالَ ابْنُ جَابِرٍ فَحَدَّثَنِي عَطَاءُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ السَّكْسَكِيُّ عَنْ كَعْبٍ أَوْ غَيْرِهِ قَالَ فَتَطْرَحُهُمْ بِالْمُهَبَّلِ قَالَ ابْنُ جَابِرٍ فَقُلْتُ يَا أَبَا يَزِيدَ وَأَيْنَ الْمُهَبَّلُ قَالَ مَطْلَعُ الشَّمْسِ قَالَ وَيُرْسِلُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ مَطَرًا لَا يَكُنْ مِنْهُ بَيْتُ وَبَرٍ وَلَا مَدَرٍ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمًا فَيَغْسِلُ الْأَرْضَ حَتَّى يَتْرُكَهَا كَالزَّلَفَةِ وَيُقَالُ لِلْأَرْضِ أَنْبِتِي ثَمَرَتَكِ وَرُدِّي بَرَكَتَكِ قَالَ فَيَوْمَئِذٍ يَأْكُلُ النَّفَرُ مِنْ الرُّمَّانَةِ وَيَسْتَظِلُّونَ بِقِحْفِهَا وَيُبَارَكُ فِي الرِّسْلِ حَتَّى أَنَّ اللَّقْحَةَ مِنْ الْإِبِلِ لَتَكْفِي الْفِئَامَ مِنْ النَّاسِ وَاللَّقْحَةَ مِنْ الْبَقَرِ تَكْفِي الْفَخِذَ وَالشَّاةَ مِنْ الْغَنَمِ تَكْفِي أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ قَالَ فَبَيْنَا هُمْ عَلَى ذَلِكَ إِذْ بَعَثَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ رِيحًا طَيِّبَةً تَحْتَ آبَاطِهِمْ فَتَقْبِضُ رُوحَ كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ أَوْ قَالَ كُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ وَيَبْقَى شِرَارُ النَّاسِ يَتَهَارَجُونَ تَهَارُجَ الْحَمِيرِ وَعَلَيْهِمْ أَوْ قَالَ وَعَلَيْهِ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ
sunnah_ref_en: Al-Walīd ibn Muslim Abū al-ʿAbbās al-Dimashqī narrated to us in Makkah by dictation saying: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd ibn Jābir narrated to me saying: Yaḥyā ibn Jābir al-Ṭāʾī, the judge of Ḥimṣ, narrated to me saying: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Jubayr ibn Nufayr al-Ḥaḍramī narrated to me, from his father, that he heard al-Nawwās ibn Samʿān al-Kilābī say: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ mentioned the Dajjāl one morning, sometimes lowering his voice and sometimes raising it, until we thought he was in a grove of palm trees. When we went to him later, he recognized that in our faces. We asked, 'O Messenger of Allah, you mentioned the Dajjāl this morning, lowering and raising your voice until we thought he was in a grove of palm trees.' He said, 'Other than the Dajjāl is what I fear more for you. If he emerges while I am among you, I will be his adversary on your behalf. If he emerges and I am not among you, then each person is his own adversary, and Allah is my successor over every Muslim. He is a young man with curly, kinky hair, one eye protruding. He will emerge from a gap between Shām and Iraq, spreading corruption right and left. O servants of Allah, hold firm!' We said, 'O Messenger of Allah, how long will he remain on earth?' He said, 'Forty days: a day like a year, a day like a month, a day like a week, and the rest of his days like your days.' We said, 'O Messenger of Allah, that day which is like a year — will one day's prayers suffice us in it?' He said, 'No, estimate its measure.' We said, 'How fast will he travel through the earth?' He said, 'Like rain driven by the wind.' He will pass by a people and call them, and they will respond. He will command the sky and it will rain, and the earth and it will grow vegetation. Their livestock will return to them with the tallest humps, the fullest flanks, and the most abundant udders. He will pass by another people and call them, but they will reject his claim. Their wealth will follow him, and they will wake up destitute. He will pass by ruins and say, "Bring forth your treasures," and its treasures will follow him like swarms of bees. He will command a man to be killed, striking him with a sword and cutting him in two, placed the distance of an archery target apart, then he will call him and he will come forward with his face beaming. While he is doing that, Allah عز وجل will send the Messiah, ʿĪsā ibn Maryam عليه السلام, who will descend at the white minaret east of Damascus, between two garments dyed with saffron, placing his hands on the wings of two angels. He will pursue him and kill him at the eastern gate of Ludd. While they are thus, Allah عز وجل will reveal to ʿĪsā ibn Maryam عليه السلام: "I have brought forth servants of Mine whom no one has the power to fight. So gather My servants to al-Ṭūr." Then Allah عز وجل will send Yaʾjūj and Maʾjūj, and they are as Allah عز وجل said: {from every elevation, descending}. ʿĪsā and his companions will beseech Allah عز وجل, and He will send worms upon their necks, and they will die like the death of a single soul. ʿĪsā and his companions will descend and find no spot on earth that is not filled with their stench and decay. ʿĪsā and his companions will beseech Allah عز وجل, and He will send birds like the necks of Bactrian camels that will carry them and throw them wherever Allah عز وجل wills.' Ibn Jābir said: ʿAṭāʾ ibn Yazīd al-Saksakī narrated to me from Kaʿb — or someone else — who said: 'They will throw them at al-Muhabbil.' Ibn Jābir said: I said, 'O Abū Yazīd, where is al-Muhabbil?' He said, 'Where the sun rises.' 'Then Allah عز وجل will send rain from which no house of hair or mud will be sheltered for forty days, and it will wash the earth until it leaves it like a mirror. The earth will be told, "Bring forth your fruit and restore your blessing." On that day, a group will eat from a single pomegranate and take shade under its rind. Milk will be blessed such that a single milking she-camel will suffice a large group of people, a cow will suffice a clan, and a sheep will suffice a household. While they are in that state, Allah عز وجل will send a pleasant wind beneath their armpits that will take the soul of every Muslim — or he said, every believer — and the worst of people will remain, fornicating like donkeys, and upon them — or he said, upon it — the Hour will come.'
iwh_id: Al-Walīd ibn Muslim Abū al-ʿAbbās al-Dimashqī menceritakan kepada kami in Makkah by dictation seraya berkata: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd ibn Jābir menceritakan kepadaku seraya berkata: Yaḥyā ibn Jābir al-Ṭāʾī, the judge of Ḥimṣ, menceritakan kepadaku seraya berkata: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Jubayr ibn Nufayr al-Ḥaḍramī menceritakan kepadaku, dari his father, that dia mendengar al-Nawwās ibn Samʿān al-Kilābī say: Rasulullah ﷺ mentioned the Dajjāl one morning, sometimes lowering his voice and sometimes raising it, until we thought he was in a grove of palm trees. When we went to him later, he recognized that in our faces. We asked, 'O Messenger of Allah, you mentioned the Dajjāl this morning, lowering and raising your voice until we thought he was in a grove of palm trees.' He berkata, 'Other than the Dajjāl is what I fear more for you. If he emerges while I am among you, I will be his adversary on your behalf. If he emerges and I am not among you, then each person is his own adversary, and Allah is my successor over every Muslim. He is a young man with curly, kinky hair, one eye protruding. He will emerge dari a gap between Shām and Iraq, spreading corruption right and left. O servants of Allah, hold firm!' We berkata, 'O Messenger of Allah, how long will he remain on earth?' He berkata, 'Forty days: a day like a year, a day like a month, a day like a week, and the rest of his days like your days.' We berkata, 'O Messenger of Allah, that day which is like a year — will one day's prayers suffice us in it?' He berkata, 'No, estimate its measure.' We berkata, 'How fast will he travel through the earth?' He berkata, 'Like rain driven by the wind.' He will pass by a people and call them, and they will respond. He will command the sky and it will rain, and the earth and it will grow vegetation. Their livestock will return to them with the tallest humps, the fullest flanks, and the most abundant udders. He will pass by another people and call them, but they will reject his claim. Their wealth will follow him, and they will wake up destitute. He will pass by ruins and say, "Bring forth your treasures," and its treasures will follow him like swarms of bees. He will command a man to be killed, striking him with a sword and cutting him in two, placed the distance of an archery target apart, then he will call him and he will come forward with his face beaming. While he is doing that, Allah عز وجل will send the Messiah, ʿĪsā ibn Maryam عليه السلام, who will descend at the white minaret east of Damascus, between two garments dyed with saffron, placing his hands on the wings of two angels. He will pursue him and kill him at the eastern gate of Ludd. While they are thus, Allah عز وجل will reveal to ʿĪsā ibn Maryam عليه السلام: "I have brought forth servants of Mine whom no one has the power to fight. So gather My servants to al-Ṭūr." Then Allah عز وجل will send Yaʾjūj and Maʾjūj, and they are as Allah عز وجل berkata: {from every elevation, descending}. ʿĪsā and his companions will beseech Allah عز وجل, and He will send worms upon their necks, and they will die like the death of a single soul. ʿĪsā and his companions will descend and find no spot on earth that is not filled with their stench and decay. ʿĪsā and his companions will beseech Allah عز وجل, and He will send birds like the necks of Bactrian camels that will carry them and throw them wherever Allah عز وجل wills.' Ibn Jābir berkata: ʿAṭāʾ ibn Yazīd al-Saksakī menceritakan kepadaku dari Kaʿb — or someone else — yang berkata: 'They will throw them at al-Muhabbil.' Ibn Jābir berkata: aku berkata, 'O Abū Yazīd, where is al-Muhabbil?' He berkata, 'Where the sun rises.' 'Then Allah عز وجل will send rain from which no house of hair or mud will be sheltered for forty days, and it will wash the earth until it leaves it like a mirror. The earth will be told, "Bring forth your fruit and restore your blessing." On that day, a group will eat dari a single pomegranate and take shade under its rind. Milk will be blessed such that a single milking she-camel will suffice a large group of people, a cow will suffice a clan, and a sheep will suffice a household. While they are in that state, Allah عز وجل will send a pleasant wind beneath their armpits that will take the soul of every Muslim — or dia berkata, every believer — and the worst of people will remain, fornicating like donkeys, and upon them — or dia berkata, upon it — the Hour will come.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبُو الْعَبَّاسِ الدِّمَشْقِيُّ بِمَكَّةَ إِمْلَاءً قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي يَحْيَى بْنُ جَابِرٍ الطَّائِيُّ قَاضِي حِمْصَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ جُبَيْرِ بْنِ نُفَيْرٍ الْحَضْرَمِيُّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ النَّوَّاسَ بْنَ سَمْعَانَ الْكِلَابِيَّ قَالَ ذَكَرَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ الدَّجَّالَ ذَاتَ غَدَاةٍ فَخَفَّضَ فِيهِ وَرَفَّعَ حَتَّى ظَنَنَّاهُ فِي طَائِفَةِ النَّخْلِ فَلَمَّا رُحْنَا إِلَيْهِ عَرَفَ ذَلِكَ فِي وُجُوهِنَا فَسَأَلْنَاهُ فَقُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ذَكَرْتَ الدَّجَّالَ الْغَدَاةَ فَخَفَّضْتَ فِيهِ وَرَفَّعْتَ حَتَّى ظَنَنَّاهُ فِي طَائِفَةِ النَّخْلِ قَالَ غَيْرُ الدَّجَّالِ أَخْوَفُنِي عَلَيْكُمْ فَإِنْ يَخْرُجْ وَأَنَا فِيكُمْ فَأَنَا حَجِيجُهُ دُونَكُمْ وَإِنْ يَخْرُجْ وَلَسْتُ فِيكُمْ فَامْرُؤٌ حَجِيجُ نَفْسِهِ وَاللَّهُ خَلِيفَتِي عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ إِنَّهُ شَابٌّ جَعْدٌ قَطَطٌ عَيْنُهُ طَافِيَةٌ وَإِنَّهُ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَّةٍ بَيْنَ الشَّامِ وَالْعِرَاقِ فَعَاثَ يَمِينًا وَشِمَالًا يَا عِبَادَ اللَّهِ اثْبُتُوا قُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ مَا لُبْثُهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ قَالَ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمًا يَوْمٌ كَسَنَةٍ وَيَوْمٌ كَشَهْرٍ وَيَوْمٌ كَجُمُعَةٍ وَسَائِرُ أَيَّامِهِ كَأَيَّامِكُمْ قُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَذَلِكَ الْيَوْمُ الَّذِي هُوَ كَسَنَةٍ أَيَكْفِينَا فِيهِ صَلَاةُ يَوْمٍ وَلَيْلَةٍ قَالَ لَا اقْدُرُوا لَهُ قَدْرَهُ قُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَمَا إِسْرَاعُهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ قَالَ كَالْغَيْثِ اسْتَدْبَرَتْهُ الرِّيحُ قَالَ فَيَمُرُّ بِالْحَيِّ فَيَدْعُوهُمْ فَيَسْتَجِيبُونَ لَهُ فَيَأْمُرُ السَّمَاءَ فَتُمْطِرُ وَالْأَرْضَ فَتُنْبِتُ وَتَرُوحُ عَلَيْهِمْ سَارِحَتُهُمْ وَهِيَ أَطْوَلُ مَا كَانَتْ ذُرًى وَأَمَدُّهُ خَوَاصِرَ وَأَسْبَغُهُ ضُرُوعًا وَيَمُرُّ بِالْحَيِّ فَيَدْعُوهُمْ فَيَرُدُّوا عَلَيْهِ قَوْلَهُ فَتَتْبَعُهُ أَمْوَالُهُمْ فَيُصْبِحُونَ مُمْحِلِينَ لَيْسَ لَهُمْ مِنْ أَمْوَالِهِمْ شَيْءٌ وَيَمُرُّ بِالْخَرِبَةِ فَيَقُولُ لَهَا أَخْرِجِي كُنُوزَكِ فَتَتْبَعُهُ كُنُوزُهَا كَيَعَاسِيبِ النَّحْلِ قَالَ وَيَأْمُرُ بِرَجُلٍ فَيُقْتَلُ فَيَضْرِبُهُ بِالسَّيْفِ فَيَقْطَعُهُ جِزْلَتَيْنِ رَمْيَةَ الْغَرَضِ ثُمَّ يَدْعُوهُ فَيُقْبِلُ إِلَيْهِ يَتَهَلَّلُ وَجْهُهُ قَالَ فَبَيْنَا هُوَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ إِذْ بَعَثَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ الْمَسِيحَ ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام فَيَنْزِلُ عِنْدَ الْمَنَارَةِ الْبَيْضَاءِ شَرْقِيَّ دِمَشْقَ بَيْنَ مَهْرُودَتَيْنِ وَاضِعًا يَدَهُ عَلَى أَجْنِحَةِ مَلَكَيْنِ فَيَتْبَعُهُ فَيُدْرِكُهُ فَيَقْتُلُهُ عِنْدَ بَابِ لُدٍّ الشَّرْقِيِّ قَالَ فَبَيْنَم
matn_ar: َا هُمْ كَذَلِكَ إِذْ أَوْحَى اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ إِلَى عِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام أَنِّي قَدْ أَخْرَجْتُ عِبَادًا مِنْ عِبَادِي لَا يَدَانِ لَكَ بِقِتَالِهِمْ فَحَوِّزْ عِبَادِي إِلَى الطُّورِ فَيَبْعَثُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ وَهُمْ كَمَا قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ { مِنْ كُلِّ حَدَبٍ يَنْسِلُونَ } فَيَرْغَبُ عِيسَى وَأَصْحَابُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَيُرْسِلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَغَفًا فِي رِقَابِهِمْ فَيُصْبِحُونَ فَرْسَى كَمَوْتِ نَفْسٍ وَاحِدَةٍ فَيَهْبِطُ عِيسَى وَأَصْحَابُهُ فَلَا يَجِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بَيْتًا إِلَّا قَدْ مَلَأَهُ زَهَمُهُمْ وَنَتِنُهُمْ فَيَرْغَبُ عِيسَى وَأَصْحَابُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَيُرْسِلُ عَلَيْهِمْ طَيْرًا كَأَعْنَاقِ الْبُخْتِ فَتَحْمِلُهُمْ فَتَطْرَحُهُمْ حَيْثُ شَاءَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَالَ ابْنُ جَابِرٍ فَحَدَّثَنِي عَطَاءُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ السَّكْسَكِيُّ عَنْ كَعْبٍ أَوْ غَيْرِهِ قَالَ فَتَطْرَحُهُمْ بِالْمُهَبَّلِ قَالَ ابْنُ جَابِرٍ فَقُلْتُ يَا أَبَا يَزِيدَ وَأَيْنَ الْمُهَبَّلُ قَالَ مَطْلَعُ الشَّمْسِ قَالَ وَيُرْسِلُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ مَطَرًا لَا يَكُنْ مِنْهُ بَيْتُ وَبَرٍ وَلَا مَدَرٍ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمًا فَيَغْسِلُ الْأَرْضَ حَتَّى يَتْرُكَهَا كَالزَّلَفَةِ وَيُقَالُ لِلْأَرْضِ أَنْبِتِي ثَمَرَتَكِ وَرُدِّي بَرَكَتَكِ قَالَ فَيَوْمَئِذٍ يَأْكُلُ النَّفَرُ مِنْ الرُّمَّانَةِ وَيَسْتَظِلُّونَ بِقِحْفِهَا وَيُبَارَكُ فِي الرِّسْلِ حَتَّى أَنَّ اللَّقْحَةَ مِنْ الْإِبِلِ لَتَكْفِي الْفِئَامَ مِنْ النَّاسِ وَاللَّقْحَةَ مِنْ الْبَقَرِ تَكْفِي الْفَخِذَ وَالشَّاةَ مِنْ الْغَنَمِ تَكْفِي أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ قَالَ فَبَيْنَا هُمْ عَلَى ذَلِكَ إِذْ بَعَثَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ رِيحًا طَيِّبَةً تَحْتَ آبَاطِهِمْ فَتَقْبِضُ رُوحَ كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ أَوْ قَالَ كُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ وَيَبْقَى شِرَارُ النَّاسِ يَتَهَارَجُونَ تَهَارُجَ الْحَمِيرِ وَعَلَيْهِمْ أَوْ قَالَ وَعَلَيْهِ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ
matn_ar_clean: ا هم كذلك إذ أوحى الله عز وجل إلى عيسى ابن مريم عليه السلام أني قد أخرجت عبادا من عبادي لا يدان لك بقتالهم فحوز عبادي إلى الطور فيبعث الله عز وجل يأجوج ومأجوج وهم كما قال الله عز وجل { من كل حدب ينسلون } فيرغب عيسى وأصحابه إلى الله عز وجل فيرسل عليهم نغفا في رقابهم فيصبحون فرسى كموت نفس واحدة فيهبط عيسى وأصحابه فلا يجدون في الأرض بيتا إلا قد ملأه زهمهم ونتنهم فيرغب عيسى وأصحابه إلى الله عز وجل فيرسل عليهم طيرا كأعناق البخت فتحملهم فتطرحهم حيث شاء الله عز وجل قال ابن جابر فحدثني عطاء بن يزيد السكسكي عن كعب أو غيره قال فتطرحهم بالمهبل قال ابن جابر فقلت يا أبا يزيد وأين المهبل قال مطلع الشمس قال ويرسل الله عز وجل مطرا لا يكن منه بيت وبر ولا مدر أربعين يوما فيغسل الأرض حتى يتركها كالزلفة ويقال للأرض أنبتي ثمرتك وردي بركتك قال فيومئذ يأكل النفر من الرمانة ويستظلون بقحفها ويبارك في الرسل حتى أن اللقحة من الإبل لتكفي الفئام من الناس واللقحة من البقر تكفي الفخذ والشاة من الغنم تكفي أهل البيت قال فبينا هم على ذلك إذ بعث الله عز وجل ريحا طيبة تحت آباطهم فتقبض روح كل مسلم أو قال كل مؤمن ويبقى شرار الناس يتهارجون تهارج الحمير وعليهم أو قال وعليه تقوم الساعة

isnad_chain.name_ar: يحيى بن جابر الطائي قاضي حمص

iwh_en: Al-Walīd ibn Muslim Abū al-ʿAbbās al-Dimashqī narrated to us in Makkah by dictation saying: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd ibn Jābir narrated to me saying: Yaḥyā ibn Jābir al-Ṭāʾī, the judge of Ḥimṣ, narrated to me saying: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Jubayr ibn Nufayr al-Ḥaḍramī narrated to me, from his father, that he heard al-Nawwās ibn Samʿān al-Kilābī say: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ mentioned the Dajjāl one morning, sometimes lowering his voice and sometimes raising it, until we thought he was in a grove of palm trees. When we went to him later, he recognized that in our faces. We asked, 'O Messenger of Allah, you mentioned the Dajjāl this morning, lowering and raising your voice until we thought he was in a grove of palm trees.' He said, 'Other than the Dajjāl is what I fear more for you. If he emerges while I am among you, I will be his adversary on your behalf. If he emerges and I am not among you, then each person is his own adversary, and Allah is my successor over every Muslim. He is a young man with curly, kinky hair, one eye protruding. He will emerge from a gap between Shām and Iraq, spreading corruption right and left. O servants of Allah, hold firm!' We said, 'O Messenger of Allah, how long will he remain on earth?' He said, 'Forty days: a day like a year, a day like a month, a day like a week, and the rest of his days like your days.' We said, 'O Messenger of Allah, that day which is like a year — will one day's prayers suffice us in it?' He said, 'No, estimate its measure.' We said, 'How fast will he travel through the earth?' He said, 'Like rain driven by the wind.' He will pass by a people and call them, and they will respond. He will command the sky and it will rain, and the earth and it will grow vegetation. Their livestock will return to them with the tallest humps, the fullest flanks, and the most abundant udders. He will pass by another people and call them, but they will reject his claim. Their wealth will follow him, and they will wake up destitute. He will pass by ruins and say, "Bring forth your treasures," and its treasures will follow him like swarms of bees. He will command a man to be killed, striking him with a sword and cutting him in two, placed the distance of an archery target apart, then he will call him and he will come forward with his face beaming. While he is doing that, Allah عز وجل will send the Messiah, ʿĪsā ibn Maryam عليه السلام, who will descend at the white minaret east of Damascus, between two garments dyed with saffron, placing his hands on the wings of two angels. He will pursue him and kill him at the eastern gate of Ludd. While they are thus, Allah عز وجل will reveal to ʿĪsā ibn Maryam عليه السلام: "I have brought forth servants of Mine whom no one has the power to fight. So gather My servants to al-Ṭūr." Then Allah عز وجل will send Yaʾjūj and Maʾjūj, and they are as Allah عز وجل said: {from every elevation, descending}. ʿĪsā and his companions will beseech Allah عز وجل, and He will send worms upon their necks, and they will die like the death of a single soul. ʿĪsā and his companions will descend and find no spot on earth that is not filled with their stench and decay. ʿĪsā and his companions will beseech Allah عز وجل, and He will send birds like the necks of Bactrian camels that will carry them and throw them wherever Allah عز وجل wills.' Ibn Jābir said: ʿAṭāʾ ibn Yazīd al-Saksakī narrated to me from Kaʿb — or someone else — who said: 'They will throw them at al-Muhabbil.' Ibn Jābir said: I said, 'O Abū Yazīd, where is al-Muhabbil?' He said, 'Where the sun rises.' 'Then Allah عز وجل will send rain from which no house of hair or mud will be sheltered for forty days, and it will wash the earth until it leaves it like a mirror. The earth will be told, "Bring forth your fruit and restore your blessing." On that day, a group will eat from a single pomegranate and take shade under its rind. Milk will be blessed such that a single milking she-camel will suffice a large group of people, a cow will suffice a clan, and a sheep will suffice a household. While they are in that state, Allah عز وجل will send a pleasant wind beneath their armpits that will take the soul of every Muslim — or he said, every believer — and the worst of people will remain, fornicating like donkeys, and upon them — or he said, upon it — the Hour will come.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي ‏ ‏ابْنَ جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏بُسْرُ بْنُ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ الْحَضْرَمِيُّ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ ‏ ‏أَبَا إِدْرِيسَ الْخَوْلَانِيَّ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏النَّوَّاسَ بْنَ سَمْعَانَ الْكِلَابِيَّ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَا مِنْ قَلْبٍ إِلَّا وَهُوَ بَيْنَ أُصْبُعَيْنِ مِنْ أَصَابِعِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ إِنْ شَاءَ أَنْ يُقِيمَهُ أَقَامَهُ وَإِنْ شَاءَ أَنْ ‏ ‏يُزِيغَهُ ‏ ‏أَزَاغَهُ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ يَقُولُ يَا مُقَلِّبَ الْقُلُوبِ ثَبِّتْ قُلُوبَنَا عَلَى دِينِكَ وَالْمِيزَانُ بِيَدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يَخْفِضُهُ وَيَرْفَعُهُ ‏
